META.DESCRIPTION: Community guidelines and rules for Coding Global
META.KEYWORDS: rules, guidelines, community standards, code of conduct
META.TITLE: Rules - Coding Global

RULE_1.TITLE: 1. Discord guidelines
RULE_1.CONTENT: The Discord Community Guidelines apply to Programming [DE / EN]; users can view these at https://discord.com/guidelines.

RULE_2.TITLE: 2. Language
RULE_2.CONTENT: The language of our community is German and English. In the voice channel, "Public [DE]" German is the primary language. Users can speak English voluntarily. However, users must speak English in the Voice Channel "Public [EN]".

RULE_3.TITLE: 3. Asking questions
RULE_3.CONTENT:
3.1 Nobody is obliged to help or answer a question. Help is generally free of charge.  
3.2 Members with the role of "HELPER" have volunteered to help with questions.  
3.3 Pinging members to expect a faster response is not allowed.  
3.4 Don't ask questions like "Does anyone know about XY?" ask the question directly, see also ➔ https://dontasktoask.com/.  
3.5 Note the thread "How do I ask a good question?" on Stackoverflow ➔ https://stackoverflow.com/help/how-to-ask.

RULE_4.TITLE: 4. Recording
RULE_4.CONTENT: The recording of videos (screen sharing, webcam) and sound (voice chat) is not permitted on this Discord.

RULE_5.TITLE: 5. Advertising
RULE_5.CONTENT: Any kind of third-party advertising is prohibited.

RULE_6.TITLE: 6. Moderators
RULE_6.CONTENT: The moderation team has the right to take appropriate actions against a user, even if a certain rule has not been explicitly violated.

RULE_7.TITLE: 7. Direct messages
RULE_7.CONTENT: Please refrain from sending direct messages to the administrators.

RULE_8.TITLE: 8. Dissemination of personal information
RULE_8.CONTENT: Dissemination of other people's personal information of any kind without their consent is prohibited and will result in a permanent ban.

RULE_9.TITLE: 9. Enforcement of a measure
RULE_9.CONTENT: The interpretation of the rules is at the discretion of the respective administrator.

RULE_10.TITLE: 10. AI Use / Spam / Job listings
RULE_10.CONTENT: The use of AI to make job listings or other spam content is not permitted. Users found doing so will be jailed, muted, or banned.

RULE_11.TITLE: 11. Fake Skills / Code Stacks
RULE_11.CONTENT: Falsely claiming to have skills or experience for the purpose of scamming or obtaining jobs is prohibited. Users must be able to prove their skills.

RULE_12.TITLE: 12. No DM
RULE_12.CONTENT: No direct messages without previous authorization. If someone reports you, you may be muted or restricted.

META.TITLE: JavaScript - Coding Global
META.DESCRIPTION: Learn JavaScript with comprehensive guides, tutorials, and best practices
META.KEYWORDS: javascript, js, web development, programming, tutorials

WHY_JAVASCRIPT.TITLE: Why JavaScript?
WHY_JAVASCRIPT.CONTENT: JavaScript is the easiest path to becoming a full-stack developer. With a single language, you can build everything from interactive websites to server-side applications, mobile apps, and even desktop software.

WEB_DEV_SPEEDRUN.TITLE: Web Dev Speedrun
WEB_DEV_SPEEDRUN.DESCRIPTION: This is the exact path I took to learn web development. Follow these resources in order to build a good foundation in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Each resource builds on the previous one.

NODEJS.TITLE: Node.js
NODEJS.CONTENT_1: Node.js lets you run JavaScript on the server, making you a true full-stack developer. With Node.js, you can build APIs, handle databases, and create complete web applications using the same language you learned for the frontend. Download it from

REACT.TITLE: React
REACT.CONTENT: Once you've learned the basics and Node.js, it's time to master React. React is the most popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces and is used by companies like Meta, Netflix, and Airbnb.

SHADCN.TITLE: Designing with shadcn/ui
SHADCN.DESCRIPTION: is a collection of beautifully designed, accessible components that you can copy and paste into your apps. It's built on top of Radix UI and Tailwind CSS, giving you full control over your components.
SHADCN.BLOCKS_DESCRIPTION: These libraries provide pre-built components and blocks that work seamlessly with shadcn/ui.
SHADCN.UTILS_DESCRIPTION: Helpful tools for customizing and extending shadcn/ui.

REACT_DOCS.TITLE: React Official Docs
REACT_DOCS.DESCRIPTION: The best place to learn React from scratch

META.TITLE: Python - Coding Global
META.DESCRIPTION: Master Python with guides, tutorials, and best practices for all skill levels
META.KEYWORDS: python, programming, tutorials, data science, automation

PYTHON.TITLE: Python
PYTHON.CONTENT_1: Python is really only relevant for specific fields like data science, machine learning, scientific computing, and automation scripting. Outside of these areas, it's not particularly useful.
PYTHON.CONTENT_2: Many people choose Python as their first language because they think it's simple. While the syntax is readable, this simplicity doesn't transfer well to other languages or real-world development. If you want to become a full-stack developer, JavaScript is a much better choice since it works on both frontend and backend.
PYTHON.CONTENT_3: But if you really want to learn Python, check out

META.TITLE: Cyber Security - Coding Global
META.DESCRIPTION: Learn cyber security fundamentals, best practices, and techniques to protect your applications
META.KEYWORDS: cyber security, security, hacking, penetration testing, infosec

WELCOME.TITLE: Welcome
WELCOME.INTRO_TEXT: Hi there! Welcome to the server. If you're new to hacking, tech, or programming, that's okay! This post is for you. This is your all-in-one resource for getting started.

ETHICS.TITLE: Legality & Ethics
ETHICS.DESC: Before you start, you must understand the law. Read up on the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act and the GDPR in the EU.

BOUNTY.TITLE: Bug Bounty Loophole
BOUNTY.DESC: Want to hack legally and get paid? Bug bounties allow ethical hackers to test participating companies within a safe scope.

LEARNING.TITLE: Learning about Computers
LEARNING.DESC: You can't hack what you don't understand. Pick a machine (Linux/Unix recommended) and learn basics via OverTheWire Bandit.

GOOGLING.TITLE: The Power of Googling
GOOGLING.DESC: Its impossible to memorize everything. Learning how to search and what questions to ask is your most important skill.

TOOLS.TITLE: Worthwhile Tools
TOOLS.DESCRIPTION: Don't worry about tools right away. Focus on understanding first. When you're ready, start with these staples.

ETHICS_DEEP_DIVE.TITLE: Ethics and Legal in Depth
ETHICS_DEEP_DIVE.CONTENT_1: Ethical hacking involves an authorized attempt to gain access to a system to identify and resolve vulnerabilities before they are exploited.
ETHICS_DEEP_DIVE.CONTENT_2: Remember: You are the good guy. Always ensure you have permission before testing any system or account.

INTRODUCTION.TITLE: Introduction
INTRODUCTION.CONTENT_1: To break a system, you have to understand it better than the person who built it. This isn't just about running scripts or hacking tools. You have to go through the meticulous process of identifying logical flaws, memory corruption, and protocol weaknesses that developers never intended to exist, taking software and infrastructure apart at the binary level to map out every possible attack surface.
INTRODUCTION.CONTENT_2: This level of depth is what separates a researcher from someone just running scripts. When you’ve manually mapped the attack surface, you see the logic errors that everyone else walked right past. That’s how real-world research and exploitation actually happen.

LEARNING_PLATFORMS.TITLE: Additional Resources
LEARNING_PLATFORMS.DESCRIPTION: These resources range from hands-on challenges, CTFs, and virtual labs where you can practice legally and safely.

TRYHACKME.TITLE: TryHackMe
TRYHACKME.DESCRIPTION: Free online platform for learning cyber security with hands-on exercises and labs

HACKTHEBOX.TITLE: Hack The Box
HACKTHEBOX.DESCRIPTION: Cyber mastery platform with beginner-friendly modules and real-world challenges

PICOCTF.TITLE: picoCTF
PICOCTF.DESCRIPTION: Free computer security education program by Carnegie Mellon University

ROOTME.TITLE: Root Me
ROOTME.DESCRIPTION: Platform for practicing hacking skills with challenges and virtual environments

CISCO.TITLE: Cisco Networking Academy
CISCO.DESCRIPTION: Learn cybersecurity, networking, and Python from Cisco's official training program

PHRACK.TITLE: Phrack.org
PHRACK.DESCRIPTION: One of the oldest E-zine in the underground hacking community.

TMPOUT.TITLE: tmp.0ut.
TMPOUT.DESCRIPTION: Hacking e-zine with a much tighter focus on low-level systems and binary exploitation.

PAGEDOUT.TITLE: Paged Out!
PAGEDOUT.DESCRIPTION: Technical magazine about programming, hacking, security hacking, retro computers, modern computers, electronics, demoscene, and other similar topics.

META.TITLE: Free AI Assistants - Coding Global
META.DESCRIPTION: The best free AI tools in 2026 for chat, coding, image generation, and research. Updated for the modern landscape.
META.KEYWORDS: ai assistants, free ai, gemini, chatgpt, claude, deepseek, opencode, nano banana, perplexity

AI_ASSISTANTS.TITLE: Free AI Assistants
AI_ASSISTANTS.DESCRIPTION: The free AI landscape moves fast. This list focuses on what actually works for free users in 2026, ordered by where the free tier still has real teeth.

CHATBOTS.TITLE: AI Chatbots
CHATBOTS.DESCRIPTION: General-purpose assistants. Gemini and DeepSeek currently offer the most generous free tiers; ChatGPT and Claude are tighter but still excellent for daily use.

GEMINI.TITLE: Gemini
GEMINI.DESCRIPTION: Google's flagship. Best free tier overall, with web search, multimodal input, and Workspace integration.

CHATGPT.TITLE: ChatGPT
CHATGPT.DESCRIPTION: OpenAI's classic. Free tier covers GPT-5 with daily caps, image generation, and web search.

CLAUDE.TITLE: Claude
CLAUDE.DESCRIPTION: Anthropic's assistant. Best free tier for writing quality and careful reasoning.

DEEPSEEK.TITLE: DeepSeek
DEEPSEEK.DESCRIPTION: Open-weight reasoning models. Free chat with no visible cap and strong coding ability.

GROK.TITLE: Grok
GROK.DESCRIPTION: xAI's chatbot. Real-time X data, generous free image generation via Grok Imagine.

MISTRAL.TITLE: Le Chat
MISTRAL.DESCRIPTION: Mistral's assistant. EU-hosted, fast, with a free tier including web search and code interpreter.

COPILOT.TITLE: Microsoft Copilot
COPILOT.DESCRIPTION: GPT-powered assistant baked into Windows, Edge, and Microsoft 365 apps.

QWEN.TITLE: Qwen Chat
QWEN.DESCRIPTION: Alibaba's open-weight family. Free chat with strong multilingual and coding performance.

CODING_ASSISTANTS.TITLE: Coding Assistants
CODING_ASSISTANTS.DESCRIPTION: Tools that write, refactor, and debug code with you. The free tier story has changed: terminal agents that bring-your-own-key (OpenCode, Aider, Cline) plus a local model or a free provider key now beat most paid editor subscriptions.

OPENCODE.TITLE: OpenCode
OPENCODE.DESCRIPTION: Open-source terminal agent. Provider-agnostic, supports 75+ models, and OpenCode Zen rotates a list of models that are free for limited periods.

CLAUDE_CODE.TITLE: Claude Code
CLAUDE_CODE.DESCRIPTION: Anthropic's terminal agent. Best-in-class reasoning when paired with a Claude Pro/Max subscription; otherwise pay-per-token.

GEMINI_CLI.TITLE: Gemini CLI
GEMINI_CLI.DESCRIPTION: Google's terminal agent. The cheapest path to high-quality AI coding: 1,000 free requests/day with Gemini's 1M-token context.

GITHUB_COPILOT.TITLE: GitHub Copilot Free
GITHUB_COPILOT.DESCRIPTION: Free tier for GitHub users. Inline completions and chat in VS Code, JetBrains, and Visual Studio.

CURSOR.TITLE: Cursor
CURSOR.DESCRIPTION: AI-first IDE. The best supervised editor experience; the free tier is limited but enough to evaluate.

CLINE.TITLE: Cline
CLINE.DESCRIPTION: Open-source VS Code extension. Bring your own key, including free providers, with a polished agentic UI.

CONTINUE.TITLE: Continue
CONTINUE.DESCRIPTION: Open-source IDE assistant for VS Code and JetBrains. Pairs cleanly with local models via Ollama.

AIDER.TITLE: Aider
AIDER.DESCRIPTION: Terminal pair-programmer with git-native workflow. Architect/editor split keeps cost low on any provider.

IMAGE_GENERATION.TITLE: Image Generation
IMAGE_GENERATION.DESCRIPTION: AI image tools you can use without paying. Nano Banana 2 inside Gemini is the strongest free pick; Flux is the best self-hosted option.

NANO_BANANA.TITLE: Nano Banana 2 (Gemini)
NANO_BANANA.DESCRIPTION: Google's image model inside Gemini. Best free quality, ~20 images/day, excellent for editing existing images.

CHATGPT.TITLE: ChatGPT Image
CHATGPT.DESCRIPTION: GPT Image inside ChatGPT. Strong text rendering, good for infographics, mockups, and marketing visuals.

FLUX.TITLE: Flux
FLUX.DESCRIPTION: Black Forest Labs' open-weight model. Run Flux Schnell or Flux Dev locally for unlimited free generation.

IDEOGRAM.TITLE: Ideogram
IDEOGRAM.DESCRIPTION: Best-in-class for text inside images. Free credits weekly. Great for posters, signs, and logos.

RECRAFT.TITLE: Recraft
RECRAFT.DESCRIPTION: Design-focused with SVG export. Free plan is for exploration only; commercial use requires the paid plan.

GROK_IMAGINE.TITLE: Grok Imagine
GROK_IMAGINE.DESCRIPTION: Inside Grok. ~10 images every 2 hours; the highest free volume of any major option.

LEONARDO.TITLE: Leonardo
LEONARDO.DESCRIPTION: Daily free credits, multiple model styles, and fine-grained controls for artists and designers.

BING_IMAGE.TITLE: Bing Image Creator
BING_IMAGE.DESCRIPTION: Free, no install. Sign in with a Microsoft account and generate with daily boost credits.

SEARCH_ENGINES.TITLE: AI Search Engines
SEARCH_ENGINES.DESCRIPTION: Search with citations and synthesized answers. Perplexity is still the benchmark; Phind is best for developers; Brave and DuckDuckGo lead on privacy.

PERPLEXITY.TITLE: Perplexity
PERPLEXITY.DESCRIPTION: Cited answers with real-time web grounding. Free tier covers most everyday research.

PHIND.TITLE: Phind
PHIND.DESCRIPTION: Built for developers. Searches docs, GitHub, Stack Overflow, and explains code with cited answers.

BRAVE.TITLE: Brave Search AI
BRAVE.DESCRIPTION: Independent index, AI answers, no tracking. Free, privacy-first, no account required.

DUCKDUCKGO.TITLE: Duck.ai
DUCKDUCKGO.DESCRIPTION: Anonymous chat with GPT, Claude, and Llama models. Zero data retention by DuckDuckGo.

KAGI.TITLE: Kagi
KAGI.DESCRIPTION: Paid, but the cleanest premium search. Strong AI assistant and universal summarizer with no ads.

YOU.TITLE: You.com
YOU.DESCRIPTION: Agent-based search with specialized modes for research, writing, and coding.

UNOROUTER_PROMO.TITLE: Want all of these models from one API?
UNOROUTER_PROMO.DESCRIPTION: UnoRouter gives you a single endpoint with intelligent routing, automatic failover, and 200+ models from 35+ providers. Subscribe for better value or pay as you go, no per-provider sign-up needed.

META.TITLE: Vibe Coding - Coding Global
META.DESCRIPTION: How to vibe code in 2026 for free: install OpenCode, pick a free model on OpenCode Zen, and ship.
META.KEYWORDS: vibe coding, opencode, opencode zen, claude code, gemini cli, free ai coding, terminal agent

WHAT_IS_VIBE_CODING.TITLE: What is Vibe Coding?
WHAT_IS_VIBE_CODING.CONTENT_1: Vibe coding is a way of programming where you describe what you want in plain language and let an AI agent do the typing. You stay in the loop reviewing diffs, running things, and steering, but you stop hand-writing every line.
WHAT_IS_VIBE_CODING.CONTENT_2: The term was coined by Andrej Karpathy in this tweet:
WHAT_IS_VIBE_CODING.CONTENT_3: The free-tier landscape changed a lot in 2025 and 2026. Editor subscriptions used to be the obvious choice; today the best free setup is an open-source terminal agent plus a free or cheap model. Here's the setup, then a tour of the alternatives.

OPENCODE_SETUP.TITLE: Best Free Setup: OpenCode
OPENCODE_SETUP.CONTENT_2: an open-source, MIT-licensed terminal agent built by the SST team. It's provider-agnostic, supports 75+ models including local ones via Ollama, and has a polished TUI plus a desktop app and IDE extensions for VS Code, Cursor, Zed, and JetBrains.
OPENCODE_SETUP.INSTALL_COMMAND:
# macOS / Linux
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash

# Or via npm
npm i -g opencode-ai@latest

# Then run
opencode
OPENCODE_SETUP.CONTENT_3: Once installed, run the /connect command and sign in with
OPENCODE_SETUP.CONTENT_4: OpenCode Zen is a curated set of models served by the OpenCode team. Some are free for limited periods. The current free lineup rotates as feedback windows close, so check the Zen page for what's available right now. If you want a stable cost-effective baseline, Anthropic's Claude (with a Pro/Max plan) and DeepSeek are the usual go-tos.
OPENCODE_SETUP.CONTENT_5: To help the agent see your codebase efficiently, pair it with
OPENCODE_SETUP.CONTENT_6: a VS Code extension that walks import graphs and packages just the relevant files into your prompt. Useful when you're switching to a model with a smaller context window.
OPENCODE_SETUP.UNOROUTER_NOTE: Want a single endpoint for every model instead of juggling provider keys? UnoRouter routes across 200+ models from 35+ providers with automatic failover.

CHOOSING_THE_BEST_MODEL.TITLE: Choosing a Model
CHOOSING_THE_BEST_MODEL.CONTENT_1: Different models are better at different things. As a rough 2026 starting point: Claude Sonnet/Opus for dense refactors and reasoning, Gemini 2.5 Pro when you need a giant context window, GPT-5 codex variants for general coding, DeepSeek V3 when you want strong quality at near-zero cost, and OpenCode Zen's currently-free models when you're experimenting.
CHOOSING_THE_BEST_MODEL.CONTENT_2: For an independent ranking that updates as new models drop, check the
CHOOSING_THE_BEST_MODEL.CONTENT_3: Don't lock in. Switching models in OpenCode is a single command, so try a few on the same task and keep whichever one wins for your stack.

AI_CLIS.TITLE: Terminal Agents (Alternatives to OpenCode)
AI_CLIS.DESCRIPTION: Other terminal agents worth knowing. The list is ordered roughly by popularity in 2026.

OPENCODE.TITLE: OpenCode
OPENCODE.DESCRIPTION: Open source. Provider-agnostic, with 75+ models and a free Zen tier that rotates.

CLAUDE_CODE.TITLE: Claude Code
CLAUDE_CODE.DESCRIPTION: Anthropic's official agent. Top-tier reasoning, especially with a Pro/Max subscription.

GEMINI_CLI.TITLE: Gemini CLI
GEMINI_CLI.DESCRIPTION: Google's official agent. 1,000 free requests/day with Gemini's 1M-token context.

OPENAI_CODEX.TITLE: Codex CLI
OPENAI_CODEX.DESCRIPTION: OpenAI's official agent. Pairs with ChatGPT Plus or pay-per-token via the API.

AIDER.TITLE: Aider
AIDER.DESCRIPTION: Git-native pair programmer. Architect/editor split keeps cost low across any provider.

CLINE.TITLE: Cline
CLINE.DESCRIPTION: Open-source VS Code extension. Bring your own key, including free providers.

AI_EDITORS.TITLE: AI-First Editors
AI_EDITORS.DESCRIPTION: If you'd rather have AI baked into a graphical editor than driven from a terminal, these are the modern picks.

CURSOR.TITLE: Cursor
CURSOR.DESCRIPTION: VS Code fork. Best supervised IDE experience, with multi-file Composer and parallel agents.

WINDSURF.TITLE: Windsurf
WINDSURF.DESCRIPTION: AI-native IDE from the Windsurf team. Cascade agent and free tier with daily message limits.

ANTIGRAVITY.TITLE: Antigravity
ANTIGRAVITY.DESCRIPTION: Google's AI-first coding environment. Plans, browses, and edits across multiple files.

UNOROUTER.AI_ASSISTANTS_PROMO_DESC: Access 200+ AI models from 35+ providers through a single API. Pay per token with no subscriptions.
